learningObjectives: Use core work and jobs vocabulary at A2 level.
Use present simple and present continuous to talk about jobs and current work situations.
Ask and answer Wh- questions about work routines and temporary tasks.
Scan a job advertisement for specific information such as salary and shift times.
Extract factual details from short job interview recordings.
Write a short email applying for a part-time job.
Answer interview questions about work experience and career plans.

grammarReference.explanation: Use present simple for habits, facts and permanent jobs (I work in a shop). Use present continuous for actions happening now or temporary situations (I am working extra shifts this month).
examples: I work in a bookshop every Saturday.
She is preparing for an interview this week.
My colleague usually starts his shift at nine.
They are hiring new staff at the moment.
commonMistakes: I am work in a café (×) → I work in a café (✓)
She works now on her CV (×) → She is working on her CV now (✓)
He is usually work on Fridays (×) → He usually works on Fridays (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use Wh- word + do/does + subject + base verb for routines and facts. Where do you work? When does your shift start? How much salary do you get?
examples: How much salary does your employer pay?
commonMistakes: Where you work? (×) → Where do you work? (✓)
When start your shift? (×) → When does your shift start? (✓)
What job you want? (×) → What job do you want? (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use Wh- word + am/is/are + subject + -ing for actions happening now. What is your colleague doing? Why are you working extra shifts?
examples: What is your colleague doing now?
Why are you working extra shifts this week?
Who is the manager interviewing today?
Where are they hiring new staff?
commonMistakes: What she is doing? (×) → What is she doing? (✓)
Why you are working late? (×) → Why are you working late? (✓)
Who the manager is interviewing? (×) → Who is the manager interviewing? (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Gist — Job Advertisement

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Job Advert
exercises.instructions: Read the advertisement. Answer about the main idea.

passage.title: Part-Time Shop Assistant — Job Advertisement
passage.text:
GREENWAY BOOKSHOP — NOW HIRING

We are looking for a friendly part-time shop assistant to join our team on weekends. The employer offers a good salary and training for the right person.

You will work with colleagues in a busy city centre shop. Duties include helping customers, organising books and working a Saturday shift from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m.

REQUIREMENTS
• At least one year of customer service experience
• Good communication skills for interviews with our manager
• Able to work on Saturday and Sunday

BENEFITS
• Hourly salary above the minimum wage
• Free books after three months
• Career advice for students who want to work in publishing

HOW TO APPLY
Send a short email with your name and work experience to jobs@greenwaybooks.co.uk before 30 April. We will contact successful candidates for an interview.

Our shop manager, Ms Pham, started here as a student. She says: "This job helped me build skills for my career."

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of this advertisement?
questions.explanation: NOW HIRING part-time shop assistant — tuyển dụng.

choices.text: To recruit a part-time shop assistant

choices.distractorNote: Ms Pham bắt đầu từ sinh viên

questions.questionText: What kind of job is offered?
questions.explanation: Part-time shop assistant on weekends.

choices.distractorNote: Publishing là lĩnh vực, không phải job title

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Job Interview
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to a job interview and extract factual information about experience, salary and shifts.

exercises.title: Learn: Café Interview
exercises.instructions: Listen to the interview. Answer the questions.

script.title: Job Interview at a Café

lines.speaker: Ms Lee
lines.text: Good morning, Tom. Thanks for coming to the interview today.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Good morning. I applied for the part-time waiter job last week.

lines.speaker: Ms Lee
lines.text: Tell me about your experience. Have you worked with customers before?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Yes. I worked in a bookshop for six months. My colleagues were very helpful.

lines.speaker: Ms Lee
lines.text: Great. The salary is eight pounds an hour. Can you work the evening shift on Fridays?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Yes, I can. I am studying now, but I am free on Friday evenings.

lines.speaker: Ms Lee
lines.text: Perfect. The employer will email you next Monday about the start date.

script.audioNotes: Clear interview dialogue, moderate pace. Approx. 50 seconds.

questions.questionText: What job did Tom apply for?
questions.explanation: Tom applied for the part-time waiter job.

questions.questionText: Where did Tom get his work experience?
questions.explanation: He worked in a bookshop for six months.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Interview
exercises.instructions: Listen again. Answer the next questions.

exercises.title: Learn: Linh's Career Call
exercises.instructions: Listen to the phone call. Answer the questions.

script.title: Career Advice and Shift Planning Call

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Hello. I want some advice about my career after school.

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: Of course. What kind of work interests you?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I like helping people. I had an interview for a nurse assistant course yesterday.

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: That is a good choice. You need more experience in a hospital. Have you thought about volunteering?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Not yet. I also want a part-time job. Can I work a weekend shift and study?

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: Many students do. Apply before September for training programmes. A Saturday shift can build useful skills.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Thank you. That helps a lot.

script.audioNotes: Friendly phone conversation, clear voices. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.explanation: Interview for a nurse assistant course yesterday.

choices.text: An interview for a nurse assistant course

script.title: Career Advice and Shift Planning Call

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Hello. I want some advice about my career after school.

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: Of course. What kind of work interests you?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I like helping people. I had an interview for a nurse assistant course yesterday.

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: That is a good choice. You need more experience in a hospital. Have you thought about volunteering?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Not yet. I also want a part-time job. Can I work a weekend shift and study?

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: Many students do. Apply before September for training programmes. A Saturday shift can build useful skills.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Thank you. That helps a lot.

script.audioNotes: Friendly phone conversation, clear voices. Approx. 45 seconds.

content.taskDescription: You saw the Greenway Bookshop job advertisement. Write an email to Ms Pham to apply for the part-time shop assistant job.

successCriteria: Clear application for the shop assistant job

modelAnswer.text:
Dear Ms Pham,

I am writing to apply for the part-time shop assistant job. I worked in a café for six months and my colleagues said I am friendly with customers. I can work the Saturday shift and Sunday shift. Could you tell me when the interview is? I would like to know more about the salary too.

Thank you,
Linh
